text:
In a quiet little town named Willowbrook, there was a little girl named Emily who loved to draw. She spent countless hours sketching mysterious creatures, crafting enchanted castles, and designing bizarre plants that could talk. One particularly snowy winter day, Emily decided to create a drawing so magical it would bring the lost forest back to life.

She spent days studying a particular tree she admired—its twisting branches seemed to hold secrets. One night, driven by her obsession, she copied the tree's twisting pattern onto paper. She hoped it would spiral into something fantastical, something that could move.

When she finally woke up the next morning, she had a drawing. But it didn't look like a tree. It had arms, wings, and a face. Emily rushed outside to admire her creation…and discovered that her drawing had come to life.

The forest came alive with birdsong and fluttering butterflies. Trees that Emily hadn't seen before popped out everywhere, weaving among the old ones like fairy guardians.
